revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
[[en:Awaga Shrine]][[ja:粟鹿神社]][[zh:粟鹿神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Awaga Jinja
粟鹿神社 | image = File:Awaga Jinja 09.JPG | caption = Haiden of Awaga Jinja {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240|qq=}} | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.300803|N|134.904811|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = 秒 | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Hoori|qid=Q1051960|1=Hoori}} | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location =2152 Awaga, Santocho, Asago-shi, Hyōgo-ken | website = | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | festival = October 17 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Asago|qid=Q720402|1=Asago, Hyōgo}} }} {{nihongo|'''Awaga Jinja'''|粟鹿神社|qq=}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the Santocho Awaga neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Asago, Hyōgo|en|Asago, Hyōgo|qid=Q720402|lt=Asago|1=Asago, Hyōgo}} in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is one of the two shrines (along with [[Izushi Shrine|Izushi Jinja]]) which claim the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Tajima Province|en|Tajima Province|qid=Q1053727|lt=Tajima Province|1=Tajima Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on October 17.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese|qq=}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Awaga Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''Amenomisari'''|天美佐利命||qq=}} * {{nihongo|'''Hiko imasu no Ōkimi'''|日子坐王命||qq=}}, the third prince of the 9th {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|qid=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika|1=Emperor Kaika}} and the great-grandfather of the 12th {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}. * {{nihongo|'''[[Hoori|Hoori]]'''|日子穂穂手見尊||qq=}}, third son of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]; grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. ==History== The origins of Awaga Jinja are unknown. According to the shrine's legend, it was founded during the reign of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}. A local noble, Ohiko Hayami offered to the Imperial Court to build a shrine to worship the Amenomisari, as this ''kami'' was an [[Mitama#Ara-mitama and nigi-mitama|''arashin'']] (rough deity) which needed placating. His great-grandson took the surname "Kambe" and was the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of Tajima. The shrine first appears in the historical documents in tax records for Tajima Province dated 737. It appears thereafter in the various historical chronicles in the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, including the ''[[Engishiki]]'' and the ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]''. The ''Engishiki'' lists it as a {{nihongo|''Myōjin Taisha''|名神大社|||qq=}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} occupied the area in 1580, he confiscated the territory of the shrine, which fell into decline. Under then [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] [[Tokugawa shogunate]], the area became {{ill|Izushi Domain|en|Izushi Domain|qid=Q10863249|lt=Izushi Domain|1=Izushi Domain}}, ruled by the [[Koide clan]] followed by the {{ill|Sengoku clan|en|Sengoku clan|qid=Q11378184|lt=Sengoku clan|1=Sengoku clan}}.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese|qq=}} During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}}, the shrine was rated as a village shrine in 1872 and promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1879 under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]].Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese|qq=}} The [[Honden]] was built in 1880 in the ''[[Nagare-zukuri]]'' style and is a three by two bay building. The [[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] is a four by three bay building. There is a small hill behind the main shrine which has what appears to be the remnants of a moat. The shrine refers to this as the "tomb of the god", but it is not certain if this is an ancient ''[[kofun]]'' or not, as it has never been excavation. However, from 1999 to 2004, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in the vicinity of the precincts due to the construction of the Kitakinki Toyooka Expressway, and the foundations of structures and a medieval stone paved approach have been found, indicating that the shrine was once much larger than it is at present. The shrine is located about 30-minutes on foot from {{ill|Yanase Station|en|Yanase Station|qid=Q870907|lt=Yanase Station|1=Yanase Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Sanin Main Line|Sanin Main Line]]. ==Gallery== Awaga-jinja, keidai.jpg|precincts Awaga-jinja, shaden.jpg|Shaden File:Awaga-jinja, honden-2.jpg|Honden File:Awaga-jinja, chokushimon.jpg|Chokushimon (Asago City ICP) File:Awaga-jinja, shinmon.jpg|Shinmon ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8|qq=}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|qid=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}, Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Awaga-jinja|qq=}} *[https://www.asabura.jp/sspot/754 Asago City home page] *[https://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6325071.html Hyōgo Jinjacho home page] ==Notes== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Shinto shrine|qq=}} {{Ichinomiya|qq=}} [[en:Awaga Shrine]] {{Commons category|Awaga-jinja}} [[ja:粟鹿神社]] [[zh:粟鹿神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Awaga Shrine (Q11604651)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tajima Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Asago District, Hyōgo|en|Asago District, Hyōgo|ja|朝来郡|ceb|Asako-gun|fa|شهرستان آساگو، هیوگو|zh|朝來郡|lt=Asago district|qid=Q4803324|1=Asago District, Hyōgo}} || {{ill|Awaga Shrine|lt=Awaga Shrine|en|Awaga Shrine|ja|粟鹿神社|commons|Category:Awaga-jinja|zh|粟鹿神社|qid=Q11604651|1=Awaga Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} || {{ill|Ni-no-Miya|qid=Q134917533|lt=Ni-no-Miya|1=Ni-no-Miya}} || — || {{coord|35.300668|134.904945|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182615 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11604651}} [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:朝来市の建築物]] [[Category:朝来市の歴史]]
